steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is the claim that the Upland South was colonized by people whose ancestors came from Scotland. This establishes a causal relationship between Scottish settlement and the linguistic similarity.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice A explains the similarity in dialects as a result of Scottish influence, aligning with the colonization history. The other options either reverse the cause-effect (C), make unsupported projections (B), or contradict the text (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
